chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Appln No.S-694 of 2025 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE. 1. For orders on M.A. No.4156/25 2. For orders on O/objection at flag-A. 3. For orders on M.A. No.4157/25 4. For hearing of bail application. 13.08.2025 Mr. Alam Sher Bozdar, Advocate alongwith applicants. ******** 1. Urgency allowed. 2. As to the office objection learned counsel states that on account of urgency said objection is overruled. Ordered accordingly. 3. Granted, subject to all just exceptions. 4. Through instant application, applicants Mukhtiar Ahmed and Imdad Ali Bozdar seek protective bail in Crime No.GO-04/2024 Police Station, ACE Sukkur under Sections 161, 465, 468, 470, 471, 477-A, 420, 34 PPC r/w Section 5 of Prevention of Corruption Act, 1947. It is, inter alia, contended that applicants are innocent and have falsely been involved in this case by the Anti-Corruption Police with malafide intention and ulterior motives. It is further contended that as soon as applicants came to know they tried to surrender themselves before the concerned Court for pre- arrest bail but applicants have grave apprehension of their being arrest before reaching the same at the hands of concerned police hence, they have approached this Court for grant of protective bail. Be that as it may, without touching merits and demerits of the case and in order to enable applicants to appear before concerned Court, they are granted protective bail for a period of 05(Five) days commencing from today, subject to furnishing their solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- each (Rupees thirty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court.

It is further contended that as soon as applicants came to know they tried to surrender themselves before the concerned Court for pre- arrest bail but applicants have grave apprehension of their being arrest before reaching the same at the hands of concerned police hence, they have approached this Court for grant of protective bail. Be that as it may, without touching merits and demerits of the case and in order to enable applicants to appear before concerned Court, they are granted protective bail for a period of 05(Five) days commencing from today, subject to furnishing their solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- each (Rupees thirty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of said period or on appearance of applicants before concerned Court, whichever is earlier. In case, applicants fail to surrender before the learned trial Court within stipulated period, their bail bond shall stand forfeited. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Ihsan/PS
